questions.name: Required Field Completeness
questions.score_label: All 5 populated
questions.evidence: model_details.name='SaProtHub SubCell Localization 650M (LoRA-fine-tuned SaProt)'; overview ~600 chars; licenses=[{identifier: 'MIT'}]; version.name='1.0'; model_parameters.model_architecture describes 'Transformer-based protein language model: 650M-parameter SaProt_650M_AF2 base...'
questions.quality_note: All five required fields populated with concrete, non-trivial content.

questions.name: Overview Length Adequacy
questions.score_label: >500 chars with structure
questions.evidence: model_details.overview describes ten-class taxonomy, LoRA fine-tuning of SaProt_650M_AF2, classifier head, and 85.75% accuracy (~600 chars). documentation field adds structured Architecture/Usage/Training Configuration sections.
questions.quality_note: Overview + documentation combined well exceed 500 chars with clear logical structure.

questions.name: Tag / Keyword Diversity
questions.score_label: >=4 tags + pipeline_tag + (language or model_category)
questions.evidence: tags=[protein-language-model, subcellular-localization, bioinformatics, protein-classification, saprot, lora, deeploc, structure-aware] (8 tags); pipeline_tag='text-classification'; language=['protein']; model_category='protein-language-model'
questions.quality_note: Eight tags plus pipeline_tag, language, and model_category — exceeds the threshold comfortably.

questions.name: Input / Output Specification
questions.score_label: Both input AND output with shape/type AND *_format_map populated
questions.evidence: input_format describes 'Structure-aware (SA) protein sequence'; input_format_map has type/encoding/max_length; output_format='Classification label (integer 0-9)'; output_format_map has shape='[B, 10]' and interpretation listing all 10 classes.
questions.quality_note: Both directions described with type, shape, and semantic interpretation.

questions.name: Schema Version Declared
questions.score_label: Pass
questions.evidence: schema_version: '0.0.2'
questions.quality_note: Recognizable schema version string declared at the top of the file.

questions.name: Persistent Identifier Present
questions.score_label: Pass
questions.evidence: model_details.path='https://huggingface.co/SaProtHub/Model-Subcellular_Localization-650M'; references include the same HF Hub URL plus the base model HF Hub URL.
questions.quality_note: HuggingFace Hub URL provides a resolvable persistent identifier.

questions.name: Funding & Acknowledgements Completeness
questions.score_label: No funding/acknowledgement mention
questions.evidence: Contributors list affiliations (Westlake Representation Learning Lab, Westlake University) but no funding agency (NIH/NSF/DOE/etc.), no grant ID, and no computing-facility acknowledgement appears anywhere in overview, mission_relevance, or contributors.
questions.quality_note: Lab affiliations are not funding/grant disclosures; nothing concrete to credit.

questions.name: Ethical & Responsible-AI Documentation
questions.score_label: Ethical considerations + bias disclosure + out-of-scope, all concrete
questions.evidence: considerations.ethical_considerations includes 'Mis-localization in drug discovery pipelines' with concrete mitigation; bias_model and bias_output narratives describe DeepLoc/SwissProt species bias and class-prior effects on rare compartments; out_of_scope_uses lists three specific scenarios.
questions.quality_note: Ethics, two bias dimensions, and three out-of-scope items — all model-specific, not boilerplate.

questions.name: License Clarity & SPDX Compliance
questions.score_label: SPDX identifier present; no explicit restriction statement
questions.evidence: model_details.licenses=[{identifier: 'MIT', license_name: 'MIT License', license_link: 'https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT'}]
questions.quality_note: Clear SPDX identifier with link, but no explicit statement about restrictions or downstream-use terms beyond the standard MIT text.

questions.name: Framework / Library Standardization
questions.score_label: Framework declared but version not strictly pinned; no model_index
questions.evidence: framework='PyTorch'; framework_version='torch>=2.0'; library_name='transformers'; no model_index field present.
questions.quality_note: Framework and library declared, but 'torch>=2.0' is a lower-bound, not a pinned version, and Papers-with-Code-style model_index is absent.

questions.name: Tool & Software Transparency
questions.score_label: Tools/libraries named but no container/env pinning
questions.evidence: framework_version='torch>=2.0'; library_name='transformers'; usage_documentation-style code snippet in documentation imports 'saprot_hub'; training_procedure mentions FP16, AdamW, LoRA configuration.
questions.quality_note: Several libraries named, but no Dockerfile, no environment lockfile, no pinned reproducibility libs.

questions.name: Training Procedure Clarity
questions.score_label: Optimizer + LR + batch + epochs + regularization disclosed
questions.evidence: training_procedure.reproducibility_info.hyperparameters: optimizer='AdamW', learning_rate=0.0005, batch_size=64, training_epochs=100, loss_function='Cross-entropy over 10 classes', optimization_techniques include LoRA rank/alpha/dropout, FP16, AdamW betas=(0.9, 0.98), weight_decay=0.01.
questions.quality_note: All required levers documented; only thing missing is an explicit LR schedule, but regularization (weight_decay, LoRA dropout) is covered.

questions.name: Version History Documentation
questions.score_label: Version name + ISO date + non-trivial diff
questions.evidence: version.name='1.0'; version.date='2024-08-01'; version.diff='Initial release of the LoRA fine-tune on the DeepLoc subcellular-localization training split using AdamW for 100 epochs.'
questions.quality_note: Semver-style name, ISO date, and meaningful change description for the initial release.

questions.name: Citations & References
questions.score_label: Multiple citations and multiple references with URLs (no DOIs)
questions.evidence: citations: 2 IEEE-style entries (SaProtHub HF page; Su et al. ICLR 2024 SaProt paper). references: 5 URLs (HF model, HF base model, GitHub SaProt, UniProt subcellular help, DeepLoc 2.0 service).
questions.quality_note: Two citations and five references, but no DOIs and citations are not in BibTeX form.

questions.name: Compute Infrastructure & Energy
questions.score_label: No compute infrastructure section
questions.evidence: No model_parameters.compute_infrastructure section present. Only an incidental 'Mixed precision (FP16) on a single GPU' note in training_procedure.description — no hardware model, no GPU-hours, no energy or carbon estimate.
questions.quality_note: Compute infrastructure block is absent; no quantitative compute or energy reporting.

questions.name: Findability (Persistent Landing)
questions.score_label: Pass
questions.evidence: model_details.path and references both contain https://huggingface.co/SaProtHub/Model-Subcellular_Localization-650M plus GitHub and base-model HF URLs.
questions.quality_note: Multiple resolvable landing pages provided.

questions.name: Accessibility & Inference Path
questions.score_label: Runnable code + I/O formats + library hook
questions.evidence: documentation contains a Python snippet using 'from saprot_hub import load_model' and model.predict(sa_sequence); input_format/output_format and *_format_map describe shape, encoding, and class interpretation; library_name='transformers'.
questions.quality_note: Concrete inference path with both the API call and explicit I/O semantics.

questions.name: Performance Metrics with Slices & CI
questions.score_label: One numeric metric, one slice, no CI
questions.evidence: quantitative_analysis.performance_metrics=[{type: 'test accuracy', value: 85.75, slice: 'DeepLoc subcellular localization test set (10-way)', unit: '%'}]
questions.quality_note: Single accuracy value on a single slice; no per-class slices, no confidence interval or error bar.

questions.name: Out-of-Scope Uses, Limitations & Tradeoffs
questions.score_label: All three populated with concrete, model-specific content
questions.evidence: limitations: 3 concrete items (SwissProt organism bias, structure-aware-input requirement, single-label only). tradeoffs: 1 item comparing 650M vs 35M variant accuracy/cost and LoRA trainable-parameter footprint. out_of_scope_uses: 3 specific scenarios (clinical diagnosis without confirmation, underrepresented species, multi-label tasks).
questions.quality_note: All three sections are populated and tied to this specific model and dataset, not generic boilerplate.

questions.name: Cross-Platform Interlinks
questions.score_label: Pass
questions.evidence: base_model='westlake-repl/SaProt_650M_AF2' links to a HF Hub record; datasets=['SaProtHub/Dataset-Subcellular_Localization-DeepLoc'] links to a sibling HF dataset; references include the SaProt GitHub repo and DeepLoc 2.0 service.
questions.quality_note: Multiple cross-platform references: base model, dataset record, and code repository.

strengths: Complete structural metadata: all required fields, rich tag set, and full I/O specification including format maps and class interpretation.
Strong responsible-AI block: concrete ethical concern with mitigation, two bias dimensions (bias_model and bias_output), three model-specific out-of-scope statements, and three concrete limitations.
Comprehensive training-procedure disclosure: optimizer, LR, batch size, epochs, loss, LoRA rank/alpha/dropout, weight decay, AdamW betas, and mixed-precision setting all recorded.
Good cross-platform interlinking via base_model, datasets, and references to the SaProt repo and DeepLoc 2.0 service.
weaknesses: No compute_infrastructure section: hardware model, GPU-hours, and energy/carbon are absent (Q15 = 0).
Performance reporting is thin: only one metric on one slice, no per-class breakdowns or confidence intervals (Q18 = 3).
Framework version 'torch>=2.0' is a lower bound rather than a pin, and no Papers-with-Code-style model_index is provided (Q10 = 3, Q11 = 3).
References list URLs but no DOIs, and citations are IEEE-style strings rather than BibTeX (Q14 = 4).
recommendations: Add a compute_infrastructure section with GPU model and count, total GPU-hours, and an energy or carbon estimate (e.g., via codecarbon).
Disclose the funder, grant ID, and any computing facility (e.g., the Westlake cluster) that supported training.
Expand performance_metrics with per-compartment accuracy/F1, macro-F1, and bootstrap 95% confidence intervals so rare-class performance is visible.
Pin framework_version to an exact PyTorch release (e.g., torch==2.1.0) and either add a Papers-with-Code-style model_index entry or a Dockerfile/lockfile reference.
Promote references to DOIs where possible (SaProt ICLR paper, DeepLoc 2.0 paper) and convert citations to BibTeX entries.
